creator: grant_watson@yahoo.com
text:
I posted this in response to one of his questions on the awesome bug tracker: 'Setting the terminal to "xterm -hold" (or running that command with 
Super+r) does keep the window open when in tiling mode.  All such 
windows remain open; some produce working shell prompts, and others are 
simply blank.'

That does begin to smell like an sh bug, especially when combined with the trouble I had with termit in ports/178194.  (It had occurred to me after reporting this bug that they might be related, but I assumed an sh bug was less likely than an incompatibility with a port.)  Perhaps sh doesn't like changes to the console dimensions early on in the process?  Terminal windows which launch successfully resize without further problems.


How do we go about discovering whether or not this is an sh issue?
